Clear plastic bottle.note: empty and rinse if possible; remove cap

Glass bottle.where: designated glass recycling point — find your nearest one at the EPD recycling map
Glass bottle.note: glass is not accepted in normal public bins; use a designated collection point

Drink carton.where: GREEN@COMMUNITY station or other designated carton recycling point
Drink carton.note: flatten, rinse, and dry first; beverage cartons are usually handled separately from paper

Paper bag.note: keep it dry; remove any plastic handles or tape

Plastic film.note: plastic film is not accepted in normal public recycling bins in HK

Other plastic.note: most mixed or unidentified rigid plastics are not accepted in normal public recycling bins

Disposable plastic cup.note: single-use plastic cups are generally not accepted in normal public recycling bins in HK

Food waste.where: designated food waste recycling point — find your nearest one at the EPD food waste map
Food waste.note: use the food waste recycling scheme if available near you

Cigarette.note: cigarette butts always go to general waste

Battery.note: never throw batteries in normal bins; use the Rechargeable Battery Recycling Programme

some sort of general waste item.note: when in doubt, general waste is always the safe fallback
